mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate has been pinned near zero (0.12%) for multiple months, reflecting sustained accommodative monetary policy. This persistently low policy rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, supporting a positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns as long as the rate stays near the zero lower bound.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.12% as of t-1mo, near zero lower bound
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or SPX produces negative mean forward returns during periods of sustained near-zero rates with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid improvement in the unemployment rate (declining more than 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month window) signals accelerating labor market recovery, which historically precedes sustained equity gains over a quarterly horizon as earnings expectations are revised upward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls more than 0.5pp over trailing 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.4% as of t-1mo, down sharply from pandemic peak near 14.7%
failure_condition: In triggered instances, 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher than unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or hit rate of positive returns falls below 55% across at least 3 independent trigger episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that jointly signals durable economic reacceleration, which is not captured by either series alone. This joint demand signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over a monthly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 436772 million as of t-1mo, HOUST at 926k units as of t-2mo, both above pre-pandemic trend
failure_condition: Joint interaction term (RSAFS growth x HOUST growth) shows Rank IC below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or the interaction model underperforms the sum of individual single-series models on out-of-sample forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) recovering steadily during a post-shock rebound regime signals restoration of supply-side capacity utilization, which supports corporate earnings recovery and positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon, but this relationship is regime-specific and does not hold in normal late-cycle expansions where INDPRO growth is mature.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 105.18 as of t-1mo, rebounding from 2020 pandemic trough
failure_condition: During the post-shock recovery window, correlation between INDPRO month-over-month growth and forward 60-day SPX returns falls below 0.05, or the relationship reverses sign outside the recovery regime without being flagged as regime failure.

mechanism_narrative: Continued positive month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects an ongoing employment recovery trend that mechanically supports consumer income and spending, translating into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon as an always-on trend signal.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 141367 thousand as of t-1mo, rising from pandemic trough
regime_hint: any regime with positive payroll momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS month-over-month change and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive PAYEMS growth periods show forward returns statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp acceleration in CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth above a threshold signals building inflationary pressure that raises expectations of future monetary tightening, causing short-term equity market weakness within a one-week horizon as investors reprice discount rates.
failure_condition: SPX shows non-negative mean 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered high-inflation-growth instances, or Rank IC is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) across triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A joint rebound in real GDP (GDPC1) alongside strong retail sales (RSAFS) growth signals a self-reinforcing demand-driven expansion where consumption growth confirms output growth, jointly predicting sustained positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon beyond what either series predicts independently.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16304.8 billion as of t-4mo reflecting Q3 2020 rebound, RSAFS at 436772 million as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Interaction model combining GDPC1 growth and RSAFS growth fails to outperform univariate GDPC1-only model on out-of-sample Rank IC, or joint signal produces Rank IC below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden decline in industrial production (INDPRO) crossing a negative monthly threshold signals an emerging supply-side contraction that raises recession risk and triggers a short-horizon negative reaction in S&P 500 returns as markets price in weaker corporate output and earnings.
failure_condition: In triggered instances of INDPRO decline exceeding threshold, SPX forward 5-day returns are not significantly negative (p > 0.05) relative to baseline, or hit rate of negative returns falls below 55% across at least 3 independent trigger episodes.
